Pull Request #5791: ⬆ Bump ruff from 0.0.138 to 0.0.183 Bumps [ruff](https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff) from 0.0.138 to 0.0.183.
Release notes

Sourced from ruff's releases.

v0.0.183

What's Changed

  • Avoid fixing E711 and E712 issues that would cause F632 by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1248
  • Implement pandas-vet by @​edgarrmondragon in charliermarsh/ruff#1235
  • Implement U016: Remove six compatibility code by @​martinlehoux in charliermarsh/ruff#1013
  • Test to prevent continious reformatting when used together with black by @​squiddy in charliermarsh/ruff#1206
  • Avoid generating invalid statements when deleting from multi-statement lines by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1253
  • Implement flake8-errmsg by @​edgarrmondragon in charliermarsh/ruff#1258
  • Avoid removing partially-unused imports by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1259

Full Changelog: https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff/compare/v0.0.182...v0.0.183

v0.0.182

What's Changed

  • Ignore any pyproject.toml without a [tool.ruff] section by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1243
  • Treat extend-* configuration options as "always extended" by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1245
  • Use more precise ranges for function and class checks by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1247

Full Changelog: https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff/compare/v0.0.181...v0.0.182

v0.0.181

What's Changed

  • Apply fix to all errors in E711 and E712 autofix by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1238
  • Avoid converting expression to statement in invald contexts by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1239
  • Automatically ignore files specified in .gitignore by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1234
  • Add new .gitignore behavior to BREAKING_CHANGES.md by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1240
  • Always check zero-depth CLI paths by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1241
  • Enable opt-out of .gitignore checks via respect-gitignore flag by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1242

Full Changelog: https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff/compare/v0.0.180...v0.0.181

v0.0.180

What's Changed

  • Apply CLI options even when no pyproject.toml is found by @​cdbrendel in charliermarsh/ruff#1232

New Contributors

  • @​cdbrendel made their first contribution in charliermarsh/ruff#1232

Full Changelog: https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff/compare/v0.0.179...v0.0.180

v0.0.179

What's Changed

  • Upgrade RustPython to support parenthesized context managers by @​charliermarsh in charliermarsh/ruff#1228

0.0.181

Files excluded by .gitignore are now ignored (#1234)

Ruff will now avoid checking files that are excluded by .ignore, .gitignore, .git/info/exclude, and global gitignore files. This behavior is powered by the ignore crate, and is applied in addition to Ruff's built-in exclude system.

To disable this behavior, set respect-gitignore = false in your pyproject.toml file.

Note that hidden files (i.e., files and directories prefixed with a .) are not ignored by default.

0.0.178

Configuration files are now resolved hierarchically (#1190)

pyproject.toml files are now resolved hierarchically, such that for each Python file, we find the first pyproject.toml file in its path, and use that to determine its lint settings.

See the README for more.

text:
Pull Request #5667: Combine inherited nonparam Security() objects

A simplified version of the rejected #5433 , this one combines the list of inherited "nonparam" dependencies.  Multiple instances of a `Security` dependency are combined, their `scopes` joined into a single scope list.

This serves two purposes:
1) In case there are many different scope checks set up, combine the invokation into one dependency call for efficiency.
2) Allow a security dependency to interpret the combined list of scopes, for example, adding semantics where specially marked scopes can _disable_ a previously added scope.

Example:
```python
def my_security(scopes: SecurityScopes):
    active = set()
    for scope in scopes.scopes:
        if not scope.startswith("-"):
            active.add(scope)
        else:
            active.discard(scope[1:])
    apply_scopes(sorted(active))
```

see also #5622 which proposes to join nonparam dependencies with parameter dependencies in a similar manner.
title: Combine inherited nonparam Security() objects

[github-actions[bot]]: This pull request has a merge conflict that needs to be resolved.

[kristjanvalur]: > @kristjanvalur, what do you think?

Hi there.

To be honest, it has been three years since this PR, and I have not been using FastAPI a lot since then.
The original idea was, IIRC, to help us wit complex security in an application, one where security scopes could be removed as well as added.

The 'collapsing' of the scopes serves to combine multiple instances security objects into one which gets all the scopes and can then make decisions on the set of scopes, rather than having each individual object working separately.

I think this is still a fundamentally good idea, since the sum of scopes evaluated at once does not have to be the same as the sum of the evaluation of individual scopes.

But as I said, its been so long and I am no longer actively using FastAPI so I am reluctant to personally pursue this further.

[YuriiMotov]: @kristjanvalur, thank you for reply!

So, I think we should close this PR (as this way the approach will be inconsistent for "non-parameter" dependencies and dependencies specified as parameters in the endpoint functions and in sub-dependencies) and focus on #5622. If we can come up with the idea on how to resolve issues that I pointed in [this comment](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/5667#pullrequestreview-3310148297), it will be interesting improvement

[kristjanvalur]: Closing this PR as per discussion.

text:
Pull Request #5472: perf: improve solve_dependencies increasing requests/s by 18%

Improve performance of function `solve_dependencies` by reducing accesses to headers, cookies and other request/response properties, which are slow in starlette. This yields and increase of requests / second of about 18%.

The PR include two changes:
The first were we use `Response(status_code=0)` to avoid creating the response with the `Content-Length` header, because accessing response.headers and mutating it is slow.

The second change checks whether there are going to be changes to the values. If not, then we don't access the request properties.

Note that the funciton `request_params_to_args` will return empty if the first argument is an empty list.

## Performance Tests
The change was tested using uvicorn with the following settings

```
uvicorn --no-access-log --no-proxy-headers test:app
```

and the following app

```py
from typing import Any

import fastapi
from fastapi import responses, routing

app = fastapi.FastAPI()

@app.get("/sync")
def sync():
    return "ok"

@app.get("/async")
async def asynch():
    return "ok"
```

### Baseline
```
$ wrk -d 30 http://localhost:8000/async
Running 30s test @ http://localhost:8000/async
  2 threads and 10 connections
  Thread Stats   Avg      Stdev     Max   +/- Stdev
    Latency     1.22ms  204.74us   8.43ms   94.62%
    Req/Sec     4.06k   273.50     7.16k    92.18%
  243045 requests in 30.10s, 29.67MB read
Requests/sec:   8074.13
```

### With the change
```
$ wrk -d 30 http://localhost:8000/async
Running 30s test @ http://localhost:8000/async
  2 threads and 10 connections
  Thread Stats   Avg      Stdev     Max   +/- Stdev
    Latency     1.04ms  307.42us  10.70ms   97.48%
    Req/Sec     4.80k   308.87     5.06k    94.67%
  286439 requests in 30.01s, 34.97MB read
Requests/sec:   9544.14
Transfer/sec:      1.17MB
```

Note that the original PR had also another change that allows it to squeeze some more performance gains, although minimal.

Instead of constructing the response as

```py
response = Response()
del response.headers["content-length"]
```

we changed it to

```py
response = Response(status_code=0)
```

which avoids creating the headers in the first place, which in turns also avoid creating a mutable copy of the headers.
title: perf: improve solve_dependencies increasing requests/s by 18%

Pull Request #5332: Fix OIDC response code when authorization header is missing

The OIDC dependency currently returns a 403 response if an `Authorization` header is missing.

Using a 401 instead of 403 aligns with the HTTP standard when authentication is missing and with the [existing OAuth2 dependency](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/b9d7f86743eb9ba2805ae0c8b99a30a3a47a58bb/fastapi/security/oauth2.py#L207-L210).

Pull Request #5163: ✨ Allow to set custom auto error detail

Allow to set custom auto error detail for HTTP Security dependencies

Sometimes it's hard to find out which dependency returns a "Not authenticated" error. It would be handy if we could set custom error detail text to easily identify the error reason. For example "auth token not provided" for one HTTP Security object and "private id not provided" for another
title: ✨ Allow to set custom auto error detail

Pull Request #5095: memo: Fixes a typo in the documentation for numeric validations

Hello there :wave: 

Thanks so much for this awesome library, which I use quite a lot whenever some back-end is involved!
I was checking the documentation about numeric validations and found a weird rendering issue:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26927750/176752051-24111354-7959-46ac-b164-603a66ad23a2.png)

The documentation section is https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/path-params-numeric-validations/#number-validations-greater-than-or-equal

I figured someone might have done an automatic replacement somewhere which caused this. This PR simply fixes that!

Any feedback is welcome!

Pull Request #4968: Customizable Request Handler in APIRoute

End goal was to provide customizable request decoding and response encoding and to forego `jsonable_encoder` in favor of one handled in my own custom serialization lib. 

This PR refactors the get_request_handler logic into an overridable class that can be passed into APIRoute which allows more options for modifying steps of request/response handling.

Typically flooded by github notifs, but I can by reached at the email on my profile if I forget to check for any updates here.
title: Customizable Request Handler in APIRoute

text:
Pull Request #4758: 🔧 Allow rough response model

for example

```python
import uvicorn

from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional
from fastapi import FastAPI


app = FastAPI()


class Item(BaseModel):
    name: Optional[str]
    price: Optional[float]


@app.get("/items/{item_id}", response_model=Item)
async def get_item(item_id: int):
    return {
        "name": "this is name",
        "price": "this is price"
    }
```

This return error by pydantic model's validator. But In many cases, the response model and the actual return value were different. So i made this PR.

This method can use like this.

```python
import uvicorn

from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional
from fastapi import FastAPI


app = FastAPI()


class Item(BaseModel):
    name: Optional[str]
    price: Optional[float]


@app.get("/items/{item_id}", response_model=Item, allow_rough_response_model=True)
async def get_item(item_id: int):
    return {
        "name": "this is name",
        "price": "this is price"
    }
```
This returns
```json
{
  "name": "this is name",
  "price": "this is price"
}
```

Look at the `allow_rough_response_model`. Default value of this is False but if set it as True, This endpoint ignore pydantic model validator.
title: 🔧 Allow rough response model

text:
Discussion on PR #4758 (🔧 Allow rough response model):

[odiseo0]: I like this.
To be clear, the way it works is that when the Pydantic model fails, instead of raising an exception, what it does is to send the dictionary as it is returned?

[tjgmlfbs124]: I like this too.

[ghost]: I have encountered many cases where this function is necessary.

[ddanier]: I'm not getting it.

The idea of setting the response model in the API endpoint is to define the contract you as the backend developer has with the client using the API. This contract in this case defines there is a field `price` thats either a `float` or `None`. If you just return a string instead your are actually breaking that contract and by doing so possibly breaking the client.

Why would you like to do that?

And if you need to have a different return type for some reason: Why shouldn't you define a new DTO response model (DTO=data transfer object) just for that case - so the API spec matches what you are really doing. Could be like so:
```python
class ItemDTO(Item):
    price: Optional[Union[float, str]]
```

Could you please explain?

[jujumilk3]: @ddanier 

Exactly right what you said all. But you know There's no Always good for conditions to dev as-is.

Like you said, define new DTO is the right way If i need little changed from original DTO. But There will be so many duplicated code if i make DTO every single time when i have to define new DTO.

For example like above, if i have to make PUT method endpoint with that DTO, i have to make new DTO without `Optional`.
like this

```python
class PutItem(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float
```

so i get two very similar DTO. Of course, in this case i can make every attrs to optional by use metaclass. But if i have to add some attr in specific endpoint, Then i have to define new class too. I can solve it by use class inheritance. Like

```python
class MoreAttr(Item):
    additional_attr: int
```

problem solved. But, if i have to define new response model with very complicated hierachy DTO? It includes n:n fields and n:1 fields. So i have to define new DTO per every model it uses.

Other frameworks have built-in options for handling DTO or Schema for these cases. (e.g: nestJS or Spring)
In this part, I Think FastAPI's pydantic Dependency works so strictly. So I just wanna to give a option for avoiding code duplication to developers.


I know the best way but, not so good for it at every cases. I'm just suggesting a new option for developers to handle this problem.
title: 🔧 Allow rough response model

text:
Pull Request #4647: 🐛 Fix `response_model_exclude_none` with Pydantic V1 when default value is not `None`

To output responses including a response_model FastAPI does something like (from my understanding):

```python
# Thats what we get from the endpoint function
response_obj = SomethingModel(...)
# See https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/master/fastapi/routing.py#L121 and https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/master/fastapi/routing.py#L76
response_data = response_obj.dict()
# See https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/master/fastapi/routing.py#L127, the field validation does just that
revalidated_obj = response_model(**response_data)
# See https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/master/fastapi/routing.py#L139, jsonable_encoder will call obj.dict() again (https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/master/fastapi/encoders.py#L52)
return jsonable_encoder(revalidated_obj.dict())
```

Now if we add response_model_exclude_none FastAPI will change this to:

```python
response_obj = SomethingModel(...)
response_data = response_obj.dict(exclude_none=True)
revalidated_obj = response_model(**response_data)
return jsonable_encoder(revalidated_obj.dict(exclude_none=True))
```

The issue now is that the `response_data` already did exclude all those `None` values. This means that when the `response_model` is then instantiated again the dictionary you use here (in my code named `response_data`) already lost those values which may cause problems.

Now if your response model is something like:
```python
class ModelNoneWithUncommonDefaults(BaseModel):
    x: Optional[str] = ...
    y: Optional[str] = "y"
```

...we have two issues with `None` values (think of `ModelNoneWithUncommonDefaults(x=None, y=None)`):
* `x` is required, but may be `None`. As `response_data` does not include `x` any more when `x == None`...the validation when instantiating `response_model` will fail with a `ValidationError`. The field has to be set.
* `y` has a default value of `"y"` but may be `None`. As `response_data` does not include `y` any more when `y == None`...the response will actually contain `y = "y"` as the validation will add its default value again. This will result in a false response.

The problem overall is the duplicate `exclude_none=True` and thus the missing values we already had when doing the validation.

The PR will skip `exclude_none` on the first conversion to `dict` and thus fix the issue. Not sure if my change was done at the best place to fix this, but I hope it is. Also the PR includes a new test to show the issue and ensure it does not come back later.

I believe `exclude_unset` and `exclude_defaults` should not have the same issue as those always have the same default values and the validation would not break/change anything. Anyways it would be cleaner to also not use those on the first conversion to `dict` I think. This has not be changed by me.
title: 🐛 Fix `response_model_exclude_none` with Pydantic V1 when default value is not `None`

text:
Pull Request #3913: Add support for Iterator in jsonable_encoder

This PR adds support for `Iterator` in the `jsonable_encoder`.

Currently, FastAPI allows for `response_model` such as `Iterable[BaseModel]` . In those cases:
1. The Pydantic Model is used to retrieve the validated data from the response object https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/20d48345465e5b09756cc9ef62fb940c63255a13/fastapi/routing.py#L129
2. The output of the validation is a List Iterator
3. The `jsonable_encoder` doesn't encode the list, but just calls `dict()` and returns a list of the fields of the `BaseModel`


By adding the support for `Iterator` in the `jsonable_encoder`, we avoid that the response object is passed to `dict()`, losing the values of the fields of the BaseModel.

An example of this issue is described in #3911 

Fixes #3911
title: Add support for Iterator in jsonable_encoder

text:
Pull Request #3874: 🐛 Fix bug preventing to use OpenAPI when using tuples

This PR is meant to fix issues (https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/3665, https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/3782) encountered generating `openapi.json` with nested models that have iterable-typed attributes (e.g. `Tuple` or `List`).

Briefly, this behavior did _not_ occur with 0.67.0, and was introduced in 0.68.0. Bisecting the issue led me to the following commit https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/commit/97fa743ecb5a716fa0223a7463cb83f3f0df494c. 

As per the error raised when using nested schemas of iterables:
```
pydantic.error_wrappers.ValidationError: 2 validation errors for OpenAPI
components -> schemas -> BuggyItem -> properties -> items -> items -> items
  value is not a valid dict (type=type_error.dict)
components -> schemas -> BuggyItem -> $ref
  field required (type=value_error.missing)
``` 
 I realized that there must be an issue when initializing the `OpenAPI` model, and specifically the `items` field of the `Schema`. This makes sense, since the commit introduced changes to the specification of the `OpenAPI` model.

And indeed, consider that we have the following `Model` to add to the openAPI specification:
```
class BuggyItem(pydantic.BaseModel):
    items: List[Tuple[str, str]]
```

We have to instantiate a `Schema` with 
```
{
  "items": {
    "title": "Items",
    "type": "array",
    "items": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": [{ "type": "string" }, { "type": "string" }]
    }
  }
}
```
In other words, the definition of the `Schema.items` attribute cannot be
```
    items: Optional["Schema"] = None
```
But must be
```
    items: Optional[Union["Schema", List["Schema"]]] = None
```
Which is the change introduced in this commit. 

Hopefully this is helpful, let me know if there is anything that I can add to this PR!
title: 🐛 Fix bug preventing to use OpenAPI when using tuples

text:
Discussion on PR #3874 (🐛 Fix bug preventing to use OpenAPI when using tuples):

[mrtolkien]: Had the issue and am waiting for a patch right now, would love to see this merged

[RileyMShea]: While this PR at least allows generation of `openapi.json` and the `/docs` endpoint to load, the nested fields themselves are unable to render. That is not unique to this PR though and the same behavior is present in FastAPI 0.67.0.  You need to provide `min_items` and `max_items` and you (sort of ) have to use a `List` or a `conlist` for a completely valid openapi.json to be created.

A `item:Tuple[int,int] = Field(...,min_items=2,max_items=2)` annotation can't be used because Pydantic thinks your type annotation takes precedence over the Field args and will throw an error about unenforced constraints.  You can silence it but then you lose on type validation.

See this example from https://pydantic-docs.helpmanual.io/usage/schema/#unenforced-field-constraints
```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, PositiveInt

try:
    # this won't work since PositiveInt takes precedence over the
    # constraints defined in Field meaning they're ignored
    class Model(BaseModel):
        foo: PositiveInt = Field(..., lt=10)
```


I had to change my Model hints to use `conlist` or `List[int]` with the min/max in Field(...)

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field,conlist

voltage: conlist(int,min_items=4,max_items=4) 
# OR
voltage: List[int] = Field(..., min_items=4,max_items=4)
```

Only then would the models properly render in the `/docs` endpoint.

[victorbenichoux]: Thanks for the comments @RileyMShea! 

Indeed, the generated `openapi.json` fails to render the `items` correctly in this case, and the `/docs` does not mention that `Tuple` is expected to be of fixed length (although it remains correctly validated when calling the endpoint, raising 422s appropriately). 

It does appear that this would in addition require a fix on the `pydantic` side which confuses fixed-length `Tuple` and variable length `List` in fields. 

That is, the following models have the same `pydantic.BaseModel.schema()` (up to the name):
```
class ListModel(pydantic.BaseModel):
    items: List[str]

class TupleModel(pydantic.BaseModel):
    items: Tuple[str]
```

When one would expect that the latter has a schema with `minLength` and `maxLength` set. I will raise an issue with them as I could not find anything related to this, and suggest a fix there.

[Mause]: @victorbenichoux that actually isn't correct - openapi has a special syntax for tuples as you correctly detailed in your original post
title: 🐛 Fix bug preventing to use OpenAPI when using tuples

Pull Request #3667: Refactor & Enhance multiple Functionality in FastAPI

- Replace if statement with if expression, Simplify if expression by using or
- Merge nested if conditions
- Simplify conditional into return statement
- Inline variable that is immediately returned
- Replace if statement with if expression, Simplify boolean if expression
- Simplify comparison to boolean, Simplify logical expression using De Morgan identities
- Replace if statement with if expression
- Merge nested if conditions, Replace unneeded comprehension with generator.
- Invert any/all to simplify comparisons
- Convert for loop into list comprehension, Inline variable that is immediately returned, Remove unnecessary else after guard condition
- Add guard clause
- Replace if statement with if expression, Replace unneeded comprehension with generator, Replace `list()`, `dict()` or `set()` with comprehension
- Replace `dict()` with `{}`
- Use `items()` to directly unpack dictionary values
title: Refactor & Enhance multiple Functionality in FastAPI

Pull Request #3038: ⬆️ Upgrade OpenAPI from 3.0.2 to 3.1.0

Following [this document](https://www.openapis.org/blog/2021/02/16/migrating-from-openapi-3-0-to-3-1-0).

## Changes
- Bump version (and check if the links are valid on the docs)
- Support `examples`

## Observations
Nothing to do on `exclusiveMinimum` and `exclusiveMaximum`, as we were already compliant based on the JSON Schema version that Pydantic uses.

Update: I got blocked by https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/5891. The latest swagger doesn't support OpenAPI 3.1.0 yet. 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7353520/113490253-2c72f200-94c9-11eb-8960-462e75ab0f96.png)

[0417taehyun]: ```suggestion **FastAPI**는 단지 개발자인, 당신에게 편의를 제공하기 위해 `fastapi.static files` 와 동일한 `starlett.static files`를 제공합니다. 하지만 사실 이것은 Starlett에서 직접 온 것입니다. ``` [0417taehyun]: ```suggestion 마운트된 응용 프로그램은 완전히 독립적이기 때문에 `APIRouter`를 사용하는 것과는 다릅니다. OpenAPI 및 응용 프로그램의 문서는 마운트된 응용 프로그램 등에서 어떤 것도 포함하지 않습니다. ``` [0417taehyun]: ```suggestion 자세한 내용은 **숙련된 사용자 안내서**에서 확인할 수 있습니다. ``` [0417taehyun]: ```suggestion "마운팅"은 특정 경로에 완전히 "독립적인" 응용 프로그램을 추가하는 것을 의미하는데, 그 후 모든 하위 경로에 대해서도 적용됩니다. ``` [0417taehyun]: ```suggestion 이 모든 매개변수는 "`static`"과 다를 수 있으며, 사용자 응용 프로그램의 요구 사항 및 구체적인 세부 정보에 따라 매개변수를 조정할 수 있습니다. ``` [0417taehyun]: ```suggestion 자세한 내용과 선택 사항을 보려면 Starlette의 정적 파일에 관한 문서를 확인하십시오. ``` [0417taehyun]: ```suggestion 첫 번째 `"/static"`은 이 "하위 응용 프로그램"이 "마운트"될 하위 경로를 가리킵니다. 따라서 `"/static"`으로 시작하는 모든 경로는 `"/static"`으로 처리됩니다. ```
title: 🌐 Add Korean translation for `docs/ko/docs/tutorial/static-files.md`

[Kludex]: @vjanz Based on what I've seen, I recommend you to:
- Create a "translation" issue e.g. https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/2041
- Divide this PR in two:
    - Language setup
    - `index.md` page
   
The reasons are:
- Being able to coordinate the translations, so others can contribute.
- To have translations merged, we need approvals from people that speaks the language. If there's a PR with only the setup, it can be merged right away and people can take their time to review the `index.md`. Besides, your PR will not block others to contribute with other pages.

[ycd]: @Kludex, i think he did it right :thinking:. Except creating a translation issue to coordinate translations :+1:

[Kludex]: Did what? 🤔

[ycd]: I mean the setup, anything wrong with it?

[Kludex]: Oh, I'm sorry. I assumed the index.md was already translated. 😔 Yeah, everything is fine!

[vjanz]: Hey @Kludex and @ycd, thank you for starting the conversation here.

To be more clear, I just did the initial setup on this PR as requested on [docs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/contributing/), and I continued translating section by section on separate branches, so other people can contribute.

So I am thinking of making a separate PR for each section that I translate if that sounds right?

Cheers!
title: 🌐 Start translation of the documentation for the Albanian language

text:
Pull Request #2337: Order routes parameters by name in OpenAPI

Currently, routes' parameters are listed in the OpenAPI docs without a specific order, other than their type:
- path
- query
- headers
- cookie

In particular, to build the OpenAPI documentation for every route, the parameters are retrieved from each route (and related dependencies), and duplicated parameters are removed with dictionary comprehension.

**Problem**
In case there are multiple parameters, it may be a lot easier to show them sorted alphabetically in the documentation (with respect their type, so **path params** before **query params** for example). Also, the current order is not specified, since dictionary elements are in insertion order and it is not really clear how to control that. A better alternative (and easier to read in the doc) is to sort them by alphabetic order.

Example of parameters, taken directly from the current tutorial's tests:
```
"parameters": [
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Q", "type": "string"},
                        "name": "q",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Skip", "type": "integer", "default": 0},
                        "name": "skip",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Limit", "type": "integer", "default": 100},
                        "name": "limit",
                        "in": "query",
                    }
                ]
```

**Solution**
To fix this problem and have the parameters for each route nicely ordered by their name, it's sufficient to sort the parameters by the alias property when they are fetched from the routes.

The above piece of OpenAPI would become:
```
"parameters": [
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Limit", "type": "integer", "default": 100},
                        "name": "limit",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Q", "type": "string"},
                        "name": "q",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Skip", "type": "integer", "default": 0},
                        "name": "skip",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                ]
```

**Caveats**
I thought about two possible caveats:
1. There may be use-cases where someone would want parameters ordered with the current logic, which is by definition (e.g. parameter A is shown before parameter B if is defined before).
2. Some images in the repo would currently not be up-to-date (the order of parameters could be different), but I don't think it should be a big problem.
title: Order routes parameters by name in OpenAPI

[graingert]: >

> Do you mind explaining what you're trying to achieve? 🎉

if you get a disconnect/connect during broadcast you can skip over sockets in the broadcast queue:

```
>>> x = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
>>> y = iter(x)
>>> next(y)
1
>>> next(y)
2
>>> next(y)
3
>>> x.remove(1)
>>> next(y)
5
```

text:
Pull Request #1922: ✨ Add support for extensions and updates to the OpenAPI schema in path operations

## Description
This PR adds support for specifying extra info for the JSON schema of path operations. This is so that [OpenAPI extensions](https://swagger.io/docs/specification/openapi-extensions/) can be used when declaring routes.

## Use case

We need this because we rely on open api extensions to configure our service mesh (linkerd) for [retries](https://linkerd.io/2/tasks/configuring-retries/) and [timeouts](https://linkerd.io/2/tasks/configuring-timeouts/), but there are probably many use cases for this.

## Implementation
- There is a lot of boilerplate to add support for extra arguments, but it does not look like there is a way around it. Suggestions welcome.
- The pydantic model of `Operation` accepts any extra info, which matches the behavior of `Path`, `Field`, `Query`, etc. There might be an argument to restrict it to `x-` prefixed fields, but this will be more complex. I'm not sure it is worth it.
title: ✨ Add support for extensions and updates to the OpenAPI schema in path operations

text:
Pull Request #853: add body to RequestValidationError for easier tracing and debugging

FastAPI is awesome, and it's schema validation is one of it's key elements. 
While debugging issues in production, the current errors data isn't sufficient to understand if it's a bad schema definition (when the API is used by multiple sources, in different ways) or really a client defect.
Adding the body data to the error can help trace problems and reproducing the issue.
title: add body to RequestValidationError for easier tracing and debugging

fix: replace innerHTML with safe DOM methods in termynal.js

text:
Discussion on PR #15381 (fix: replace innerHTML with safe DOM methods in termynal.js):

[github-actions[bot]]: ## 📝 Docs preview

Last commit 2fa969d91f8760dc01f8cdbb05e12a835ecd1077 at: https://48447688.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev

### Modified Pages

* https://48447688.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev/js/termynal.js - ([before](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/js/termynal.js))

[YuriiMotov]: @beejak, could you please explain the possible attack vector?

The thing is that this data comes from trusted source (approved by maintainers and merged).
And, if you are worrying about the docs previews - this just doesn't help. The only defense is to review changes carefully  before opening docs preview.

Anyway, thanks for your interest!

[beejak]: Thank you for the feedback!

You're right that merged/approved docs content is trusted. The specific vector I had in mind is **fork PR previews**: docs preview CI runs on pull requests from forks *before* the content is reviewed. A contributor PR with a malicious `:::termynal` block would execute JavaScript via `innerHTML` in the preview environment — in the commenter's browser, without any maintainer ever approving the content.

Using `textContent` / `createElement` instead means that class of injection simply cannot reach the DOM regardless of what the preview is rendering.

Happy to add a test case or expand the description if that makes the tradeoff clearer. Closing is also fine if the fork-preview risk is considered acceptable for this project.
title: fix: replace innerHTML with safe DOM methods in termynal.js

text:
Pull Request #13993: 📝 Add permalinks to headers in English docs

This PR adds permalinks to all headers of English pages in docs.
Permalinks were added using script.

The following edge cases are handled by script:
* exclude [non-translated sections](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/blob/9c7abbff43121bca35bec3156a428105e98c0324/scripts/docs.py#L30-L39)
* handle comments inside code blocks
* only add permalink if not exist (ensure second run will not add second permalink)
* handle html-tags in headers (use only visible text to generate permalink)

This PR also:
* bumps `mkdocs-macros-plugin` to `1.3.9` ([commit](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/13993/commits/119441d6d826688d350b51204b4650b2195f4164)) - **should I open a separate PR for this commit?**
* patches `mkdocs_hooks.py` to avoid conflict with `attr_lists` extension ([commit](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/13993/commits/ab836d290c24b7e57127e08bf916d7a2f2de9e79))
title: 📝 Add permalinks to headers in English docs

text:
Pull Request #13649: 📝 Add Vercel to cloud providers

👋 Hey y'all, saw Vercel wasn't listed here. We have folks deploying their FastAPI backends on Vercel. Let me know if you have any questions.
title: 📝 Add Vercel to cloud providers

text:
Discussion on PR #13649 (📝 Add Vercel to cloud providers):

[JefferyHus]: No please.

[yezz123]: I don't think Vercel can be a good place to deploy a fully backend app especially FastAPI one

[leerob]: @JefferyHus can you explain? We have [some documentation here](https://vercel.com/guides/hosting-backend-apis) on common use cases.

@yezz123 any more details would be appreciated. We do have customers deploying FastAPI.

[yezz123]: Hey @leerob 👋🏻 

So as a clear idea, fastAPI is built on Starlette and designed to leverage asynchronous Python patterns for high-performance API development. However, when deployed to Vercel:

1. **Cold start penalties hit harder** - Python runtimes have longer initialization times than Node.js in serverless environments, making every cold start more noticeable to users.
   
2. **Execution timeouts become problematic** - Complex database operations or third-party API calls can easily exceed Vercel's function timeout limits (10s on hobby plans) (not really well good to run some open source tests for example).

3. **Websocket limitations** - FastAPI's websocket support doesn't play nicely with Vercel's serverless model

4. **Database connection management gets tricky** - The stateless nature of serverless functions makes connection pooling difficult, leading to potential bottlenecks.

i faced this with vercel multiple times trying to give it some Python code:

- Having to artificially split APIs into smaller functions to avoid timeout issues
- Difficulty implementing background tasks that run longer than the execution limit


meanwhile i find it good for documentations like [mkdocs](https://www.mkdocs.org/) to be deployed in vercel as you can see a good example in one of my repos [yezz123/authx](https://authx.yezz.me/)

[JefferyHus]: @leerob  here’s what I based my comment on:

- Just because FastAPI can run in Vercel’s function-style model doesn’t mean it’s the right fit. You lose most of FastAPI’s powerful async and ASGI features.
- Vercel’s `server-like concurrency` is still function-based, not process-based. That’s not true ASGI behavior.
- Cold starts are only "mitigated" in perfect cases, not completely removed. And Python gets less benefit from Fluid Compute than Node.js.
- FastAPI works best when containerized and deployed on general platforms like Docker, Kubernetes, Fly.io, or Railway. Vercel locks you into its own ecosystem.

Let me know if that makes sense or if you want me to explain more.
title: 📝 Add Vercel to cloud providers

text:
Pull Request #13329: 🩺 Unify the badges across all tutorial translations

Currently, the displayed badges can be quite different between translations, even potentially showing a different status, like is the case right now: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/ vs. https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/bn/

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/423d111c-d240-47eb-80a5-dda5b36ea6a1)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9fb1f268-84d2-4bcc-827b-923cc0f0d088)

This PR ensures consistency of the displayed badges:

- Using only pushes to `master` to determine the status of the `Test` badge
- Use the new format as introduced in #13313 for all languages
- Using `coverage-badge.samuelcolvin.workers.dev` for the `Coverage` badge
- Including a badge for the supported Python versions on all pages.
title: 🩺 Unify the badges across all tutorial translations

[alejsdev]: Thanks for the contribution! @codingjenny :sparkles:
And thanks for the review @mattwang44 :100:

[mattwang44]: ```suggestion
**FastAPI CLI** 是一個命令列程式，能用來運行你的 FastAPI 應用程式、管理你的 FastAPI 專案等。
```

[mattwang44]: ```suggestion
FastAPI CLI 接收你的 Python 程式路徑（例如 `main.py`），並自動檢測 FastAPI 實例（通常命名為 `app`），確定正確的引入模組流程，然後運行該應用程式。
```

[mattwang44]: ref: https://hackmd.io/@l10n-tw/glossaries
```suggestion
預設情況下，**auto-reload** 功能是禁用的。它也會在 IP 位址 `0.0.0.0` 上監聽，表示會監聽所有可用的 IP 地址，這樣任何能與該機器通信的人都可以公開存取它。這通常是你在生產環境中運行應用的方式，例如在容器中運行時。
```

[mattwang44]: ```suggestion
你可以在[部署文件](deployment/index.md){.internal-link target=_blank}中了解更多相關資訊。
```

[yychanlee]: Thanks for your reference.

[mattwang44]: ```suggestion
`fastapi` 命令列程式就是 **FastAPI CLI**。
```

[mattwang44]: ```suggestion
預設情況下，**auto-reload** 功能是禁用的。它也會在 IP 位址 `0.0.0.0` 上監聽，表示會監聽所有可用的 IP 地址，這樣任何能與該機器通信的人都可以公開存取它。這通常是你在生產環境中運行應用程式的方式，例如在容器中運行時。
```

[mattwang44]: ```suggestion
預設情況下，**auto-reload** 功能是啟用的，當你對程式碼進行修改時，伺服器會自動重新載入。這會消耗較多資源，並且可能比禁用時更不穩定。因此，你應該只在開發環境中使用此功能。它也會在 IP 位址 `127.0.0.1` 上監聽，這是用於你的機器與自身通訊的 IP 位址（`localhost`）。
```

[k94-ishi]: 元のドキュメントでは斜体ではなく太字になっていると思いますが、バージョン違いですかね。
（ほかの部分ではソースと一致してそうですが、確認してみてください。）
一応enのソースに合わせて`**FastAPI**`のように太字にした方が良いと思います。

ソース：[https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/blob/ea0cdd120c39b34aca6ea592832d7fa43944d34d/docs/en/docs/tutorial/bigger-applications.md?plain=1#L5](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/blob/ea0cdd120c39b34aca6ea592832d7fa43944d34d/docs/en/docs/tutorial/bigger-applications.md?plain=1#L5)

[k94-ishi]: タイトルはかなり意訳されていると思いますが、`Multiple Files`の要素は残したほうが良いと思います。

[k94-ishi]: 原文の`**FastAPI** provides a convenience tool` 「FastAPIは便利なツールを提供する」
とすこし意味が離れているように感じます。文末のみ書き換えて
`**FastAPI** は、柔軟性を保ちつつ、アプリケーションを構築できる便利なツールを提供します。`
とすると原文の構造に近づくかと思います。検討してみてください。

[k94-ishi]: 「ファイルをインポートする」という表現が少しわかりにくいように感じました。
```suggestion
例えば、`app/main.py`で以下のようにインポート文を記述できます。
```
としてみてはいかがでしょうか。

[k94-ishi]: 原文の方一行抜けてますがこれもバージョン違いですかね。
編集箇所に関しては最新版に沿わないと混乱を招く恐れがあるので、この文についても追加の必要があると思います。
https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/blob/ea0cdd120c39b34aca6ea592832d7fa43944d34d/docs/en/docs/tutorial/bigger-applications.md?plain=1#L46

[k94-ishi]: `It contains an `app/main.py` file.`が訳されていないですね。
直前で指摘させていただいた、一行を補えば意味が通るように訳せると思います。

[k94-ishi]: r抜け
`app/outers/users.py` -> `app/routers/users.py`
title: 🌐 Add Japanese translation for `docs/ja/docs/tutorial/bigger-applications.md`

[tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit a03ad2aa8bc9bc974ebf340146bf8d9a8afc0dbb at: https://c05c2fad.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev

[voltagex]: Any chance this can make it into a patch release? https://github.com/encode/starlette/releases/tag/0.38.2 will silence an annoying Snyk alert that isn't mentioned in the changelog (see https://github.com/encode/starlette/discussions/2653)

[Kludex]: > Any chance this can make it into a patch release? [encode/starlette@`0.38.2` (release)](https://github.com/encode/starlette/releases/tag/0.38.2) will silence an annoying Snyk alert that isn't mentioned in the changelog (see [encode/starlette#2653](https://github.com/encode/starlette/discussions/2653))

Wouldn't Snyk continue to complain anyway? 🤔 Or was it because Starlette removed the pin of anyio on the test requirements?

[Kludex]: > I ran the tests (with Starlette 0.38.1) and did not observe any regressions.

I didn't add anything that would break FastAPI on 0.38.0 as well, FYI.

[voltagex]: > > Any chance this can make it into a patch release? [encode/starlette@`0.38.2` (release)](https://github.com/encode/starlette/releases/tag/0.38.2) will silence an annoying Snyk alert that isn't mentioned in the changelog (see [encode/starlette#2653](https://github.com/encode/starlette/discussions/2653))
> 
> Wouldn't Snyk continue to complain anyway? 🤔 Or was it because Starlette removed the pin of anyio on the test requirements?

You know, now I'm not sure. I guess I'll find out?

[jarey]: Could this be included in 0.112.1?

[Jaza]: +1

0.38.0 included https://github.com/encode/starlette/pull/2583 , which is a (non-critical but niggling) issue that's still currently affecting my apps running fastapi / starlette.

It's nice to see someone step up and complete this translation, which will help others. Thank you @camigomezdev [tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 74aa87a7d45261d011a04e70dd2c1cc8ad994762 at: https://c58184f8.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev [tiangolo]: > Además, normalmente en la documentación se ha dejado la palabra "library" en inglés, ya que la traducción "librería" es incorrecta. No sé si se debería ajustar eso en este caso, ya que la palabra se usa varias veces. Thoughts? @tiangolo 🤔 Sip, de acuerdo con @alejsdev, tal cual todo 💯 Gracias! 🤓 [camigomezdev]: Quedo atenta si hay mas comentarios :) [tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 246b6d0ad145dc5a5efbd2d852a6f94cd39c7843 at: https://a1c69998.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev [camigomezdev]: @alejsdev Estoy pendiente si hay alguna actualización adicional para hacer :) [tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 65693b5be77fad2c6e2989f9008f3f670cb3bb25 at: https://cb0d308b.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev [alejsdev]: Hola! :nerd_face: Subí unos pequeños ajustes que había pasado por alto (https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/11697/commits/65693b5be77fad2c6e2989f9008f3f670cb3bb25) :sweat_smile: Gracias a los dos por la ayuda @camigomezdev @ajpinedam :smile_cat: :v: [alejsdev]: ```suggestion Como **FastAPI** está basado en el estándar **ASGI**, es muy fácil integrar cualquier librería **GraphQL** que sea compatible con ASGI. ``` [alejsdev]: ```suggestion Tú puedes combinar *operaciones regulares de ruta* de la librería de FastAPI con GraphQL en la misma aplicación. ``` [alejsdev]: ```suggestion Asegúrate de evaluar sí los **beneficios** para tu caso de uso compensan las **desventajas.** 🤓 ``` [alejsdev]: ```suggestion Sí necesitas o quieres trabajar con **GraphQL**, **Strawberry** es la librería **recomendada** por el diseño más cercano a **FastAPI**, el cual es completamente basado en **anotaciones de tipo**. ``` [alejsdev]: ```suggestion Dependiendo de tus casos de uso, podrías preferir usar una librería diferente, pero si me preguntas, probablemente te recomendaría **Strawberry**. ``` [alejsdev]: ```suggestion Puedes aprender más sobre Strawberry en la documentación de Strawberry. ``` [alejsdev]: ```suggestion Esto fue deprecado de Starlette, pero sí aún tienes código que lo usa, puedes fácilmente **migrar** a starlette-graphene3, la cual cubre el mismo caso de uso y tiene una **interfaz casi idéntica.** ```

Pull Request #11194: 🐛 Fix validation error when `File` is declared after `Form` parameter

This PR updates the fix (6185a92) from @jsg921019 so that tests pass with the latest FastAPI code (0.110.0).
It also adds a test to cover the case with multiple files, and another to ensure the order of parameters in an endpoint definition does not affect the way FastAPI handles the request.

**Problem**
When declaring an endpoint which accepts a `File` but also a `Form` parameter, the `File` parameter must be declared first or the endpoint would respond with error 422.

**Context**
- See discussion #9116
- And probably also #4625
title: 🐛 Fix validation error when `File` is declared after `Form` parameter

I don't get what you mean or refer to

I'm talking about `elif` block:
```python
        elif (
            is_bytes_sequence_field(field)
            and isinstance(field_info, params.File)
            and value_is_sequence(value)
        ):
```
It handles list of files and it was also modified. So, I think it should be covered by tests

```suggestion
from typing_extensions import Annotated
```

```suggestion
body_create_file_files__post = {
    "title": "Body_create_file_files__post",
    "required": ["token", "file"],
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "token": {"title": "Token", "type": "string"},

    },
}


def test_openapi_schema():
    response = client.get("/openapi.json")
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    json_response = response.json()
    assert (
        json_response["components"]["schemas"]["Body_create_file_files__post"]
        == body_create_file_files__post
    )
```
We can make it more readable

text:
Discussion on PR #10807 (🌐 Add Portuguese translations for `learn/index.md` `resources/index.md` `help/index.md` `about/index.md`):

[nazarepiedady]: @Kludex, could you review this pull request, please?

[tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 0ac0c50990b1ec4fc5cc431b6790b2cbc94c3df2 at: https://7ce3fd62.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev

[nazarepiedady]: @tiangolo, could you review and apply it?

[tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit b82f8c8d7ab6d132cdb3455f8f9ea710d1f6b41e at: https://56e76680.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev

[tiangolo]: Maybe @codespearhead could help review this?

[nazarepiedady]: @tiangolo, it is taking too much time to review it, do not you think?

But I understand

[nazarepiedady]: @codespearhead, it is all resolved.

[tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit b353a04e69fdb5b9ecf9fd9e9524858578e1b58c at: https://fe3c414b.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev

[alejsdev]: Thank you all for the help! :rocket: :sparkles:  @nazarepiedady @codespearhead @SamuelBFavarin

[codespearhead]: 1. Change `a FastAPI` to `o FastAPI`.
2. Change `seu desenho` to ~~`sua arquitetura`~~ `seus padrões`.

[codespearhead]: 1. Change `Ajuda` to `Ajude`.
2. Change `envolva-se` to `participe`.

[codespearhead]: Change `aprendermos FastAPI` to `aprendermos como usar o FastAPI`.

[codespearhead]: Change `a FastAPI` to `o FastAPI`.

[codespearhead]: Change `Recursos adicionais, ligações externas` to `Material complementar, links externos`.

[alejsdev]: I don't think the second suggestion is necessary, since the official docs say "design".

[codespearhead]: What do you think @gustavoprezoto @SamuelBFavarin @EliasOlie ?

Perhaps it's a matter of dialect difference, but as a Northern Brazilian, the term `desenho` seems out of place in this context. To me, `seu desenho` suggests something more akin to the layout of the FastAPI GUI (hypothetically speaking), whereas ~~`sua arquitetura`~~ `seus padrões` conveys the idea of the documentation providing a comprehensive guide to the framework's best practices.

For reference:

- `Software Architecture` -> `Arquitetura de Software`
- `Software Design` (aka `Design Patterns`) -> `Padrões de Software` (vulgo `Padrões de Projeto`)
- ([Difference between them](https://stackoverflow.com/a/704909))

[nazarepiedady]: @codespearhead, *links* is not Portuguese, do you not have any other solution?

[nazarepiedady]: **FastAPI** is an abstraction layer over the Python programming language to facilitate the development of Web APIs or Web applications.

The **abstraction** is translated to **abstração**, so from there comes the feminine defined article. But if **framework** would be the preferred word here, we'll fall in the same solution because **framework** is translated for many as **estrutura**, but **abstração** is more accurate since the framework itself offer something more than a simple structure.

@codespearhead.

[nazarepiedady]: I will do it, but there is a point about the usage of masculine defined article.

[nazarepiedady]: @codespearhead, but the other meaning of **desenho** is **projeto**, even people that are not a native Portuguese-speaking but learned it know that.
title: 🌐 Add Portuguese translations for `learn/index.md` `resources/index.md` `help/index.md` `about/index.md`

Pull Request #10552: 📝 Add location info to `tutorial/bigger-applications.md`

This will help the reader when reading [Bigger Applications](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/bigger-applications/), which is about structuring a FastAPI app in multiple modules. So it uses code examples for multiple modules.

This PR adds a comment on top of each module, denoting the location of the current module (commit 1). The code highlights in the documentation are updated accordingly (commit 2).

#10554 is a translation of this.

[AlertRED]: > Что-то кнопки "апрув" нет... Пойду спать пока, может появиться потом. ПС. Ах, ПР закрыт.

Да, решил переделать :) Постарался организовать ПР-ы "по феншую", скоро Себастьян их добавит в Russian translation. Внесу решение по вашему предложению в новый ПР.

[Xewus]: ```suggestion
* Извлечь сохранённые данные.
```
Наверное при перечислении действий лучше использовать одинаковое склонение.

[Xewus]: ```suggestion
* Поместить эти данные в Pydantic модель.
```

[Xewus]: ```suggestion
    Чтобы отличить модели со всеми необязательными значениями для **обновления** от моделей с обязательными значениями для **создания**, можно воспользоваться идеями, описанными в [Дополнительные модели](extra-models.md){.internal-link target=_blank}.
```
Страница. на которую ведёт ссылка уже "переведена" .  :-/

Но в любом случае, в квадратных скобках можно переводить.

[Xewus]: Учитывая, что выше рассматривалось 2 вида обновлений, то с такой строкой ожидается, что ниже тоже будет два "резюме".  Но такого нет.
Может что-то вроде - "Кратко о применении обновлений"...

[Xewus]: ```suggestion
Если необходимо выполнить частичное обновление, то очень полезно использовать параметр `exclude_unset` в методе `.dict()` модели Pydantic.
```

[Xewus]: Я бы всё же перевёл `dict` как `словарь`, а 66 и 68 строки объединил в одну, так как это одна непрерывная последовательность действий.

[Xewus]: ```suggestion
## Полное обновление с помощью `PUT`
```
Или "Замена".
К ому же в 29 строке написано "замена".

[Xewus]: Аналогично комментарию к заголовку.

Pull Request #10311: 🌐 Fix typos in Russian translations for `docs/ru/docs/tutorial/background-tasks.md`, `docs/ru/docs/tutorial/body-nested-models.md`, `docs/ru/docs/tutorial/debugging.md`, `docs/ru/docs/tutorial/testing.md`

- В разделе "фоновые задачи" параметр `q` используется в качестве query-параметра, а не в значении очереди, как это описано внизу примера.
- В разделе "тестирование" используется местоимение "вы" и другие его производные с большой буквы. Причины исправления:
  1. "Вы" - это уже уважительная форма слова "ты", писать с большой буквы не имеет смысла, если это не начало предложения;
  2. Во всей остальной документации тоже используется написание этого слова с маленькой буквы.

Остальные исправления - это исправления опечаток.

[tiangolo]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 5fcf4158348bc2197ed64654e0cdb84f407cab1f at: https://5294c8ca.fastapitiangolo.pages.dev

[Xewus]: > * В разделе "фоновые задачи" параметр `q` используется в качестве query-параметра, а не в значении очереди, как это описано внизу примера.
> * В разделе "тестирование" используется местоимение "вы" и другие его производные с большой буквы. Причины исправления:
>   
>   1. "Вы" - это уже уважительная форма слова "ты", писать с большой буквы не имеет смысла, если это не начало предложения;
>   2. Во всей остальной документации тоже используется написание этого слова с маленькой буквы.
> 
> Остальные исправления - это исправления опечаток.

Касательно претензии к "Вы" (так как именно так везде пишу).
Не разу не видел и не слышал про массовые случаи чтения документации количеством людей более одного, но писать "ты" не считаю уместным, а "вы" - это множественное число.
И да, Вы правы,"Вы" - уважительная форма обращения, которая закрепилась в английском языке, равно как и вопрос - "Как Ваше имя?", а не "как тебя звать [холоп]?"

Тем не менее, несмотря на свой принцип (и правила этикета) обращаться к незнакомым людям на "Вы", я не выказываю претензий к остальным коллегам по столь незначительному поводу, так как понятна лень людей, не дающая зажать шифт или дотянуться до буквы "Ё" и в целом невысокий средний уровень образованности (впрочем, я сам не профессор).

На эту тему можно много холиварить и даже пулять источниками с обеих сторон, но вместо этого лучше перевести ещё пару страниц.
http://new.gramota.ru/spravka/letters/51-rubric-88

title: 🎨 Fix types for lifespan, upgrade Starlette to 0.26.1

text:
Discussion on PR #9245 (🎨 Fix types for lifespan, upgrade Starlette to 0.26.1):

[github-actions[bot]]: <!-- NETLIFY DEPLOY COMMENT GENERATED BY ACTIONS_NETLIFY - APP ID SHA256: 594fac83bb279707b446e5a5e3d5d816698376e1bdf2391af31124cf64b0a402 -->
🚀 Deployed on https://640fd26830532a16cd315587--fastapi.netlify.app

[github-actions[bot]]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 2e373fa10313cf6d1c9049c2905722317650cc52 at: https://640dbed4fbc9f768d2b35cd4--fastapi.netlify.app

[adriangb]: You also need to add `self: AppType`

[tiangolo]: Good point, thanks @adriangb! Trying it locally I realized I had to redefine `AppType` to be bound to `FastAPI` as well.

[adriangb]: Hmm I’m surprised the `bound=Starlette` should work and the fact that you have `self: AppType` should make the AppType always match `self` which is going to be a FastAPI instance.

[github-actions[bot]]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 4578cfbe68366311f6dca5d5454ac18a09773fde at: https://640fd2b293def71708f521d2--fastapi.netlify.app

[tiangolo]: I'm trying with this example:

```Python
from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

from fastapi import FastAPI


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(_: FastAPI) -> AsyncGenerator[None, None]:
    ...
    yield
    ...


app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
```

And I get this error:

```
mypy sand.py
fastapi/applications.py:112: error: "AppType" has no attribute "openapi_schema"
fastapi/applications.py:113: error: "AppType" has no attribute "openapi_url"
fastapi/applications.py:114: error: "AppType" has no attribute "title"
fastapi/applications.py:115: error: "AppType" has no attribute "version"
fastapi/applications.py:126: error: "AppType" has no attribute "dependency_overrides"
fastapi/applications.py:153: error: "AppType" has no attribute "setup"
Found 6 errors in 1 file (checked 1 source file)
```


...I guess it would be fine for external things to have `AppType` be bound to `Starlette`, but for the internals of `FastAPI` it seems to be a problem. But I guess it would be fine then to have the custom `AppType` here.

[adriangb]: Weird, we even have a test in Starlette: https://github.com/encode/starlette/blob/8965f8849977b5752e415722a83aa00049f80ff3/tests/test_applications.py#L539-L550

[tiangolo]: Ah, yeah, the error is not in the lifespan, but that the class doesn't have the custom attributes `FastAPI` defines.
title: 🎨 Fix types for lifespan, upgrade Starlette to 0.26.1

Just to confirm, @abhint, do you speak Russian? Just because the idea with translation reviews is to check that the translation is correct, so you need to speak the language to be able to review it. :nerd_face:

[dimastbk]: "разбор и анализ" - слово `парсинг` вполне используется в русском языке (см. поиск по google.books, например).

[dimastbk]: ```suggestion
В APIStar была система внедрения зависимостей, которая тоже требовала предварительную регистрацию компонентов, как и ранее описанные инструменты.
```

[dimastbk]: "были проблемы с безопасными интеграциями" - мне кажется, имеется ввиду что-то другое. Отсутствие интеграций защиты?

[Xewus]: Да, но всё же развернул этот термин.
Мне "парсинг" встречается обычно в контексте сбора информации с сайтов. Технически - это тоже "разбор и анализ", но боюсь, что у неискушённого читателя (а вся документация довольно лояльна к новичкам) могут возникнуть затруднения.

[Xewus]: Тут надеюсь на помощь сообщества с понятным переводом.
Данный фреймворк с рассматриваемым функционалом существовал до осени 2018 года.
И вероятно проблема обозначена здесь: https://github.com/encode/apistar/issues/515?ysclid=le46pjmr37357939435

По-видимому имеется ввиду внедрение в схему OpenAPI всяких паролей и токенов для автоматической авторизации из Swagger UI. Это нынче просто работает в FastAPI  и я не могу оценить - почему были какие-то проблемы в то время.

Может быть можно написать - " проблемы авторизации используя UI"...
title: 🌐 Add Russian translation for `docs/ru/docs/alternatives.md`

text:
Pull Request #5856: Add overloads to param functions

Add overloads to param functions to catch typing errors on a dependency/route level declaration.

```py
def sync_function() -> int:
    return 1


async def async_function() -> int:
    return 1


def sync_generator_function() -> Iterator[int]:
    yield 1


async def async_generator_function() -> AsyncIterator[int]:
    yield 1

reveal_type(Depends(sync_function))
reveal_type(Depends(async_function))
reveal_type(Depends(sync_generator_function))
reveal_type(Depends(async_generator_function))
reveal_type(Depends())
```
```py
example.py:24: note: Revealed type is "builtins.int"
example.py:25: note: Revealed type is "builtins.int"
example.py:26: note: Revealed type is "builtins.int"
example.py:27: note: Revealed type is "builtins.int"
example.py:28: note: Revealed type is "Any"
```

Before this PR we weren't able to catch errors like this:
```py
from typing import Any

from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends

app = FastAPI()


async def get_params() -> dict[str, int | str]:
    return {}


@app.get("/")
def my_route(
    params: dict[str, str] = Depends(get_params),   # incorrect type here
) -> Any:
    pass
```
title: Add overloads to param functions

Sorry for that. I'll close this one for now to keep things clean. Thank you! :cake:

[kigawas]: @tiangolo Do you have time reviewing this?

[github-actions[bot]]: 📝 Docs preview for commit d9c0a0ed8d108fdca9ad191a53facd311bdd60f6 at: https://63d9eb1cac75a43967061a63--fastapi.netlify.app

[github-actions[bot]]: 📝 Docs preview for commit 4ddc69498fd37c2f94ba5ad4ed3a25ddd489a791 at: https://63e49deae77003532f2fdb5a--fastapi.netlify.app

[tiangolo]: Thanks @kigawas! As this could hit corner cases not covered, I would rather not do it and instead do the current official way to convert dataclasses to dicts.

If you want to squeeze performance, you could serialize directly and return a response directly. Also, a future version of FastAPI will use Pydantic's own serialization, which is equivalent to orjson (Rust's SerDe), so that will probably help a lot. You would probably also get performance improvements by using Pydantic v2 with FastAPI in most places instead of dataclasses.

Given that, I'll pass on this one, but thanks for the interest! ☕
title: Revamp response serialization performance

Pull Request #5739: ⬆️ Bump Starlette from 0.22.0 to 0.23.0

## Changes

- Remove `websocket_route` decorator in favor of `websocket` from the test suite.
- Add `on_event`, `middleware` and `exception_handler` middlewares on `FastAPI`.
- Add `route` and `on_event` on `APIRouter`.
- Change Starlette range from `==0.22.0` to `>=0.22.0,<=0.23.0` - I know that you pin Starlette because minimal changes there can break FastAPI, but I'm making the range wider just for you to evaluate if it makes sense.

## Preemptive questions you may have when reviewing it

- Sebastián: Do I want to reimplement the decorator methods as it's in this PR, or should I define the decorators, and still call starlette i.e. `super().middleware()`, but suppress the `DeprecationWarning`s with a warning filter?

EDIT: I think I shouldn't have removed the `websocket_route`, but actually implemented it :sweat_smile: You'll see the coverage.
title: ⬆️ Bump Starlette from 0.22.0 to 0.23.0

text:
Pull Request #5240: 🔧 Update package metadata, drop support for Python 3.6, move build internals from Flit to Hatch

## Background

Hello there! The Python packaging ecosystem has standardized on the interface for build backends ([PEP 517](https://peps.python.org/pep-0517/)/[PEP 660](https://peps.python.org/pep-0660/)) and the format for metadata declaration ([PEP 621](https://peps.python.org/pep-0621/)/[PEP 631](https://peps.python.org/pep-0631/)).

So, I'm spending my free time updating important projects so that they are modernized and set an example for others 😄

The is a follow-up to https://github.com/samuelcolvin/pydantic/pull/4282

## Summary of changes

### Metadata

This implements [PEP 621](https://peps.python.org/pep-0621/) and removes Flit as a development dependency, allowing `pip` to be used directly which simplifies the contributing workflow and CI/CD. The build backend `hatchling` (of which I am a maintainer in the [PyPA](https://github.com/pypa)) is now used as that is the default in the official [Python packaging tutorial](https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/tutorials/packaging-projects/). Hatchling is available on all the major distribution channels such as [Debian](https://salsa.debian.org/python-team/packages/hatchling), [Fedora](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/python-hatchling), [Arch Linux](https://archlinux.org/packages/community/any/python-hatchling/), [conda-forge](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/hatchling), [Nixpkgs](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/blob/master/pkgs/development/python-modules/hatchling/default.nix), [Alpine Linux](https://github.com/alpinelinux/aports/blob/master/community/py3-hatchling/APKBUILD), [FreeBSD](https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-ports/blob/main/devel/py-hatchling/Makefile), [Gentoo Linux](https://github.com/gentoo/gentoo/tree/master/dev-python/hatchling), [MacPorts](https://github.com/macports/macports-ports/blob/master/python/py-hatchling/Portfile), [OpenEmbedded](https://github.com/openembedded/openembedded-core/commit/846e806181f1349be29cbce78c5041735dfd7e6f), [Spack](https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/develop/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/py-hatchling/package.py), etc.

### Python support

The earliest supported Python 3 version of Hatchling is 3.7, therefore I've also set that as the minimum here. Python 3.6 is EOL and other build backends like `flit-core` and `setuptools` also dropped support. Python 3.6 accounts for 3-4% of downloads in the last month: https://pypistats.org/packages/fastapi

If support is still desired for some reason, I can keep the existing cap and only require 3.7+ in CI/CD.

### Documentation

I updated the contributing docs, which accounts for most of the diff. Luckily, they were written in such a way that going from `flit` to `pip` didn't alter semantics for any language!

## Future

- We now have the ability to ship optional wheels compiled with Mypyc, as Black [recently did](https://ichard26.github.io/blog/2022/05/31/compiling-black-with-mypyc-part-1/), to be even faster in [benchmarks](https://github.com/TechEmpower/FrameworkBenchmarks/pull/7178). Though as @tiangolo mentioned offline, it may be best to wait for the great work @samuelcolvin is doing on Pydantic v2 to be over to be sure we don't cause any disruptions in their test suite.
- We could source the version from Git
title: 🔧 Update package metadata, drop support for Python 3.6, move build internals from Flit to Hatch

Pull Request #4820: ⬆ Upgrade version required of Starlette from `0.19.1` to `0.20.4`

Runs against Starlette's ~master branch~ 0.20.0.

This version drops Python 3.6.

---

Edit by @tiangolo:

Upgrade Starlette version to `0.20.4`, update imports for `ExceptionMiddleware` from the new location in Starlette.

Starlette is updated directly to `0.20.4` here as those 4 patch versions had several bug fixes, including security ones, this way it's possible to skip that, upgrade to the next feature version (from 0.19 to 0.20) and get all the recent bug fixes.

Support for Python 3.6 was just dropped in a previous PR. This PR also includes removing Pytest markers that were only needed for Python 3.6.
title: ⬆ Upgrade version required of Starlette from `0.19.1` to `0.20.4`

Pull Request #2631: 🌐 Add Japanese translation for `docs/ja/docs/advanced/conditional-openapi.md`

This PR translates advanced/conditional-openapi.md in Japanese.

This is my first PR to FastAPI docs by referring to https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/contributing/#existing-language . If I do any mistake, please let me know!

Also, I found [this comment](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1572#issuecomment-694344684) in an issue. Let me try to review the open PRs for Japanese translation since a lot of parts are already done but not reviewed in progress.

Pull Request #2564: 📝 Add link to article in Russian "FastAPI: знакомимся с фреймворком"

Hi there! 👋 

Hope it would be considered as useful addition!

In my article in Russian I covered how to:
- get familiar with FastAPI framework
- set up tests
- connect to async Tortoise ORM 
- make async test for the previous feature

Sorry in advance for the ugly link :( Gatsby generates it based on Russian headline and I still hadn't fix this.
title: 📝 Add link to article in Russian "FastAPI: знакомимся с фреймворком"
